title: IDF Strikes Multiple Tehran Neighborhoods; Tabriz Petrochemical Hit — Oil Surges to ~$115
detail: US and Israeli aircraft conducted overnight and morning strikes on multiple locations in Tehran on March 29 (Day 30), killing at least 2 people and wounding 14 in residential areas near Shaft City, Saadat Abad neighborhood, and western Tehran districts. The strikes also hit the Tabriz Petrochemical Company in northwestern Iran, sending Brent crude prices up to approximately $115/barrel on the news. A Washington Post investigation published March 29 confirmed that four of Iran's key ballistic missile manufacturing sites and at least 29 ballistic missile launch sites have been damaged across the first four weeks of war. Additional strikes targeted an IRGC complex for ballistic missile and satellite launcher R&D, and Iranian air defense systems, with power blackouts reported in parts of Tehran and Karaj.

title: Iranian Missile Hits Ne'ot Hovav Chemical Plant in Israel — Hazmat Risk
detail: An Iranian ballistic missile struck a chemical plant in the Ne'ot Hovav industrial zone in southern Israel on March 29, injuring one person and raising fears of a hazardous materials leak. Emergency response teams were dispatched to the industrial area to assess the chemical hazard. The strike marks Iran's continued targeting of industrial infrastructure inside Israel despite IDF/CENTCOM claims of near-total degradation of Iran's missile launch capacity. Israeli authorities ordered precautionary shelter-in-place measures for nearby communities pending assessment of any toxic leak.

title: Iran Targets ALBA Bahrain and Emirates Global Aluminium — Gulf Industrial Strikes Escalate
detail: Iranian missiles and UAVs struck the ALBA aluminum plant in Bahrain, injuring 2 employees, and caused significant damage to Emirates Global Aluminium in Abu Dhabi, wounding 6 workers. Iran's IRGC stated both facilities were targeted due to alleged US military-aerospace ties. The strikes represent a significant escalation in Iran's targeting of Gulf economic infrastructure beyond oil and gas — attacking aluminium plants that supply global automotive, aerospace, and packaging supply chains. Saudi Arabia intercepted 10 Iranian drones on March 29; Kuwait's National Guard shot down 4 drones; UAE air defenses responded to multiple simultaneous threats.

title: Trump Floats Kharg Island Seizure in Financial Times Interview
detail: President Trump told the Financial Times on March 29: 'To be honest with you, my favourite thing is to take the oil in Iran. Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't. We have a lot of options. I don't think they have any defence.' The statement — the most explicit presidential signal yet of a possible US seizure of Iran's primary oil export terminal on Kharg Island — caused immediate alarm among Gulf allies and sent oil traders scrambling. Trump also told reporters aboard Air Force One that Iran agreed to 'most of' the 15 US demands conveyed via Pakistan: 'They gave us most of the points. Why wouldn't they? They're agreeing with us on the plan.' Iranian state media immediately contradicted this, quoting a senior security official saying Iran formally rejected the plan.

title: Four-Nation Islamabad Summit Pushes for US-Iran Talks — Pakistan Secures 20-Ship Hormuz Deal
detail: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, and Saudi Arabia met in Islamabad on March 29 to pursue de-escalation, with Pakistan reconfirming its readiness to host direct US-Iran talks 'in coming days.' Pakistan separately announced it had secured Iran's agreement allowing 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz at a rate of 2 per day — a modest but concrete humanitarian concession. US Secretary of State Rubio told reporters the US has made 'concrete progress' in diplomatic talks and cited 'a growing amount of energy' flowing through the Strait of Hormuz. He described diplomacy as 'an ongoing and fluid process' while confirming it remains 'unclear who will even speak for the Iranians' in formal negotiations.

title: IRGC Issues Ultimatum Over University Strikes — Threatens Gulf Campus Attacks
detail: The IRGC issued a formal ultimatum on March 29 demanding the US condemn reported strikes on Iranian universities by March 30 or face expanded attacks. The IRGC specifically threatened to target American and Israeli-affiliated universities in the Gulf region — including Texas A&M Qatar, Northwestern University Qatar, and NYU Abu Dhabi — warning people within 1 km of those campuses to evacuate. The threat reflects a new Iranian escalation strategy designed to leverage Western soft-power presence in the Gulf against US allies hosting American educational institutions, following IDF strikes on the University of Science and Technology in Tehran.

title: CENTCOM: Iran Missile Launch Rate Down 90%; 393 Attack Waves Since Day 1
detail: US Admiral Brad Cooper reported on March 29 that Iran's ballistic missile launch rate had fallen 90% from Day 1 of the conflict; drone launch rates fell 83%. Total Iranian attack profile since February 28: 393 attack waves, totaling 414 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, and 1,914 UAVs in regional campaigns targeting Israel, Gulf states, and US installations. Despite the degradation in launch capacity, Iran's pre-stockpiled arsenal continues to generate effective strikes — a 10% residual rate still represents dozens of potential attacks per day. CENTCOM assessed Iran's missile production capability has been effectively destroyed, meaning remaining capacity reflects pre-war stockpiles.

title: USS Tripoli Arrives with 3,500 Marines — Potential Kharg Island / Hormuz Ground Force
detail: Approximately 3,500 US Marines aboard the USS Tripoli (LHA-7) arrived in the region on March 29, substantially increasing US amphibious and ground assault capability in the theater. Analysts noted the deployment is consistent with a Kharg Island seizure mission or Hormuz enforcement operation — both scenarios Trump publicly referenced on March 29. The Washington Post separately reported the Pentagon is preparing for 'weeks of ground operations,' potentially including special forces raids and conventional infantry, short of a full-scale invasion. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned any US ground invasion would be 'met with force.'
